finding: call_mcp_tool uuid-branch used the model-supplied server_id directly, skipping the accessible-set check the legacy path enforced — a prompt-injected tool name could execute on a server the user's groups aren't assigned to, using that server's admin secret headers (cross-group authz bypass)
a tool execution error propagated via ? and aborted the whole turn, leaving the persisted tool_use with no tool_result → next-turn history is an orphan tool_use the provider rejects

cross-request approval resume double-executed: the RegistryBridge runs MCP before_llm_call which itself executes approved tools on resume, while the loop's native resume-execution (last_pending_assistant) also executed them from stale history → tool ran twice then re-prompted forever
a partial suspend (mixed auto+prompt tools in one round) broke on the first suspend, stranding later tools with neither a result nor a pending row → orphaned tool_use + lost approval on resume
the denial check failed OPEN — a transient DB error on get_denied_tools_for_branch fell through to decide_pure, so in AutoApprove mode a denied tool could auto-execute
the single-use approval claim discarded delete_tool_approval's bool — a concurrent resume could read the same approved row and both return Auto (double-execute a mutating tool)

is_trusted name-fallback used builtin_server_id_by_name (which includes code_sandbox/control_mcp) instead of is_builtin_server_id — a name-prefixed code_sandbox tool would auto-approve code execution (latent; name path unreachable today)

the spawned generation task releases the slot + terminal frame + cancel token explicitly at the tail with no RAII/Drop guard — a panic in the task permanently latches begin_generation ('reply already generating') + leaks the cancel-token entry + hangs the SSE stream
new finish_reason wire strings for cap stops (max_steps/token_cap/timeout) are outside the legacy vocabulary (stop/cancelled) a client may switch on — cosmetic, chat caps effectively never fire

chat imports its MCP-call chokepoint (call_mcp_tool/resolve_tool_server/McpCallScope/CancelSignal) from workflow::dispatch — two sibling feature modules coupled laterally; call_mcp_tool is shared MCP infra that belongs in mcp/, not workflow

split_tool_name + mcp_to_agent_result are copy-pasted between chat resolver.rs and workflow agent_dispatch.rs and have already diverged (chat computes terminal from audience; workflow hardcodes terminal:false)

namespaced-tool __-splitting is re-implemented in 4 places (resolver split_tool_name, gate split_server_tool, workflow split_tool_name, call_mcp_tool uuid-parse) with no single owner for the <server_id>__<tool> convention
call_mcp_tool takes 9 positional params incl a bare enforce_conversation_disabled:bool (a security gate) + two Option<String> — boolean/Option soup, trivial to transpose or flip the gate silently
ChatAgentTurn is a 16-public-field struct with no constructor; provider/model_name/model_id/provider_id must stay mutually consistent (one create_provider_from_model_id call) but nothing enforces it
a gate-suspended turn emits Stopped(Halted) which the sink maps to finish_reason 'cancelled', conflating user-stop with awaiting-approval — StopReason has no Suspended variant
AgentExtension::on_delta is never called by the loop (streaming drives only DeltaSink); dead trait method that misleads the next porter
Budget per_step_token_cap / step_over_cap are never consulted by the loop (only unit-tested) — dead accounting carried through every Budget::new third arg

AgentTurnRequest.inputs / TurnContext.inputs (DEC-19) is threaded end-to-end but consumed by no host (both pass Null; chat carries its request via RegistryBridge.send_request) — over-engineered seam with zero current consumer
AgentEvent::ToolNotification is handled by the sink but never emitted by the loop (ToolProvider has no sink access) — dead variant + dead sink branch
